perturbation.original_text: 5. Term . This Agreement shall remain in effect for a two -year term (subject to a one year extension if the parties are still discussing and considering the Transaction at the end of the second year). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during term shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term . This Agreement shall remain in effect for a two -year term (subject to a one year extension if the parties are still discussing and considering the Transaction at the end of the second year). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during term shall remain in effect for the term of the agreement only.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause stating that the duty to hold confidential information remains in effect 'indefinitely'. By changing it to 'for the term of the agreement only,' the Receiving Party's confidentiality obligation expires after the agreement's two-year (or three-year) term, generating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term . This Agreement shall remain in effect for a two -year term (subject to a one year extension if the parties are still discussing and considering the Transaction at the end of the second year). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during term shall remain in effect for the term of the agreement only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5, specifically the clause regarding the Receiving Party's duty to hold confidential information. Originally, this duty was to remain in effect 'indefinitely'. The changed text limits this duty to 'the term of the agreement only'. This directly contradicts the original intent of perpetual confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 5 itself, as the Contradicted Text and Changed Text is in the same location. The explanation provided clearly indicates the contradiction stemming from changing 'indefinitely' to 'for the term of the agreement only.'

perturbation.original_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall immediately return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible mater ial embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of t he foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request ; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies . Alternatively, the Receiving Party, with the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall immediately return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible mater ial embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of t he foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request ; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as it deems necessary for any reason. Alternatively, the Receiving Party, without the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).
perturbation.explanation: The first change modifies the 'provided however' clause to allow the Receiving Party to retain documents it 'deems necessary for any reason', contradicting the original clause that limited retention to documents necessary for 'reasonable document retention policies'. This creates an in-text contradiction. The second change says that the Receiving Party, without the written consent of the Disclosing Party may destroy the documents, contradicting the statement before saying the the Receiving Party needs the written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall immediately return and redeliver to Disclosing Party all tangible mater ial embodying any Confidential Information provided hereunder and all notes, summaries, memoranda, drawings, manuals, records, excerpts or derivative information deriving therefrom, and all other documents or materials (“Notes”) (and all copies of any of t he foregoing, including “copies” that have been converted to computerized media in the form of image, data , word processing , or other types of files either manually or by image capture) based on or including any Confidential Information, in whatever form o f storage or retrieval, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings between the parties contemplated hereunder; (ii) the termination of this Agreement; or (iii) at such time as the Disclosing Party may so request ; provided however that the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as it deems necessary for any reason. Alternatively, the Receiving Party, without the written consent of the Disclosing Party may (or in the case of Notes, at the Receiving Party’s option) immediately destroy any of the foregoing embodying Confidential Information (or the reasonably nonrecoverable data erasure of computerized data) and, upon request, certify in writing such destruction by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party supervising the destruction).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding the return of confidential information. Originally, the Receiving Party could retain documents necessary for 'reasonable document retention policies.' The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain documents it 'deems necessary for any reason.' This creates a contradiction because the original text provides a specific reason for retention, while the changed text provides a much broader, less restricted reason. This broadens the scope of permissible retention in a way that is incompatible with the original, more limited scope, resulting in an in-text contradiction that makes the obligations unclear. The second change, allowing destruction without the Disclosing Party's consent, directly contradicts the original requirement of written consent, further strengthening the contradiction. The Contradicted Text is located in the same section as the Changed Text which shows that the changes made cause the contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Warranty . NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER . The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluatio n of a Transaction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information is being made by the Disclosing Party. Further, neither party is under any obligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it chooses not to disclose. Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclo sure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Warranty . NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER, except that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information provided. The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluatio n of a Transaction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information is being made by the Disclosing Party. Further, neither party is under any obligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it chooses not to disclose. Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclo sure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information provided', this directly contradicts the initial statement 'NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER'. Now, there is a specific warranty being made, leading to an in-text contradiction about whether any warranties exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Warranty . NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER, except that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information provided. The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluatio n of a Transaction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information is being made by the Disclosing Party. Further, neither party is under any obligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it chooses not to disclose. Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclo sure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'NO WARRANTIES ARE MADE BY EITHER PARTY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT WHATSOEVER.' The changed text introduces an exception to this by adding 'except that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information provided.' This creates a direct contradiction because the initial statement denies all warranties, while the added clause introduces a specific warranty. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and shows how the added text goes against the original sentiment of the section.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Miscellaneous . (a) This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous understandings and agreements, whether ora l or written, between the parties , with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement can only be modified by a written amendment signed by the party against whom enforcement of such modification is sought.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Miscellaneous . (a) This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous understandings and agreements, whether ora l or written, between the parties , with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement can be modified by email confirmation between both parties, or by a written amendment signed by the party against whom enforcement of such modification is sought.
perturbation.explanation: The change in 11. (a) adds a method of modification by 'email confirmation between both parties' in addition to a 'written amendment signed by the party against whom enforcement of such modification is sought'. This generates a contradiction because it means that the agreement can be modified in a way that is not a signed written addendum.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Miscellaneous . (a) This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous understandings and agreements, whether ora l or written, between the parties , with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement can be modified by email confirmation between both parties, or by a written amendment signed by the party against whom enforcement of such modification is sought.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 11. (a) introduces 'email confirmation' as a method to modify the agreement, in addition to a 'written amendment'. The explanation confirms that this creates a contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and it still causes a contradiction within the document because the agreement can now be modified by email and by a written addendum, which creates a contradiction of methods in modifying the contract. This adheres to the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because it defines inconsistent modifications to the contract, making interpretation unclear.
